content: By 2025, self-supervised world model memory agents can reframe core agent design by tying memory retention to emergent curiosity-driven "episodic value" rather than fixed objective function weighting: only world state transitions that trigger intrinsic surprise are permanently encoded into long-term agent memory, creating a self-organizing knowledge hierarchy that grows more aligned with an agent's open-ended goals over time. This design eliminates the common problem of catastrophic forgetting of high-impact rare events that plagues generic memory-augmented agents today.
